---
title: "Formal cutevents method 2 — GPU fix"
subtitle: 'Joint $J/\psi\to\omega\pi^+\pi^-$ / $J/\psi\to\omega K^+K^-$ amplitude fit'
date: 2026-06-20
---

::: {.callout-warning}
Preliminary working result. This page is a model and diagnostic record for the
GPU-fixed fit, not a validated final physics result.
:::

## Overview

This page summarizes the `Formal_cutevents_method2_gpu_fix` fit result for the
two-channel coupled analysis. The run used `MultiConfig(total_same=False)`: the
same $J/\psi\to\omega R$ production variables and selected line-shape
parameters can be shared across the two final states, while full-chain complex
coefficients remain channel-specific.

| Item | Value |
|---|---:|
| Condor cluster | `12984995` |
| Requested starts | 20 |
| Complete optimizer records | 10 |
| Converged optimizer records | 8 |
| Selected record | start 19 |
| Best converged NLL | **-14273.239183** |
| Floating parameters | 108 |
| Saved parameters | 270 |
| Fit-script elapsed time | 25 h 02 min 15 s |
| Peak resident memory | 18.20 GiB |
| Imported figures | 32 |

Ten starts left complete optimizer summaries. Eight converged, while two
reported precision loss. The selected converged start 19 is lower than the next
converged minimum by $\Delta\mathrm{NLL}=15.12$, so the result still depends
meaningfully on multi-start selection.

## Data and likelihood

| Channel | Data entries | Sum of sWeights | Normalization PHSP | Treatment |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| $\omega\pi^+\pi^-$ | 65,696 | 31,549.44 | 519,946 reconstructed events | p6C sample, sWeighted |
| $\omega K^+K^-$ | 65,696 | 45,276.64 | 429,521 reconstructed events | sWeighted |

Both likelihood terms use reconstructed and selected PHSP for normalization.
The $\omega K^+K^-$ channel enables tf-pwa `cached_shape`; the
$\omega\pi^+\pi^-$ channel uses the standard amplitude path. Both use
center-of-mass kinematics, `r_boost: true`, TensorFlow graph execution and 200
plotting bins.

For channel $c$, the fitted intensity is built from coherent active decay
chains,

$$
\mathcal A_c(x)=\sum_r c_{c,r}\,\mathcal M_{c,r}
  (x;\theta_c,\theta_{\rm shared}),\qquad
I_c(x)=\sum_{\rm helicities}|\mathcal A_c(x)|^2.
$$

The two normalized, sWeight likelihood terms are added,

$$
-\log\mathcal L_{\rm joint}
=\sum_c\alpha_c\left[-\sum_{i\in D_c}w_{ci}\log I_c(x_{ci})
+\left(\sum_{i\in D_c}w_{ci}\right)\log\widehat{\mathcal N}_c\right],
$$

$$
\alpha_c=\frac{\sum_i w_{ci}}{\sum_i w_{ci}^2},\qquad
\widehat{\mathcal N}_c=
\frac{\sum_{j\in {\rm PHSP}_c}v_{cj}I_c(y_{cj})}{\sum_j v_{cj}}.
$$

Ordinary resonances use tf-pwa's default relativistic Breit-Wigner with running
width. Nonresonant states configured with `model: one` use a constant line
shape. The shared $f_0(980)$ and $f_2(1270)$ use the configured two-channel
Flatte form with $\pi\pi$ and $K\bar K$ thresholds.

## Active amplitude model

### $\omega\pi^+\pi^-$: 15 coherent chains

| Subsystem | Active states |
|---|---|
| $\omega\pi^+$ | $b_1(1235)^+$, nonresonant $0^-$, $\rho(1450)^+$, $\rho(1690)^+$ |
| $\omega\pi^-$ | charge-conjugate partners of the four $\omega\pi^+$ states |
| $\pi^+\pi^-$ | nonresonant $2^+$, $f_0(500)$, $f_0(980)$ Flatte, $f_2(1270)$ Flatte, $f_0(1370)$, $f_0(1500)$, $f_2(2300)$ |

The configured $f_0(1525)\to\pi\pi$ chain is rejected by tf-pwa's LS check
and is not active. The commented $\rho(1570)$ and $f_0(1710)$ hypotheses are
also inactive.

### $\omega K^+K^-$: 12 coherent chains

| Subsystem | Active states |
|---|---|
| $\omega K^+$ | $K_2(2250)^+$, $K_1(1400)^+$ |
| $\omega K^-$ | $K_2(2250)^-$, $K_1(1400)^-$ |
| $K^+K^-$ | nonresonant $0^+$, $f_0(980)$ Flatte, $f_2(1270)$ Flatte, $f_0(1370)$, $f_0(1500)$, $f_2(1525)$, $f_0(1710)$, $f_2(2150)$ |

### Cross-channel sharing

| Shared resonance family | Shared between channels | Still channel-specific |
|---|---|---|
| $f_0(980)$ | $J/\psi\to\omega f_0(980)$ production LS variables, Flatte mass and $g_{\pi\pi}, g_{K\bar K}$ | Full-chain complex totals for $f_0(980)\to\pi\pi$ and $f_0(980)\to K\bar K$ |
| $f_2(1270)$ | $J/\psi\to\omega f_2(1270)$ production LS variables, Flatte mass and $g_{\pi\pi}, g_{K\bar K}$ | Full-chain complex totals for $f_2(1270)\to\pi\pi$ and $f_2(1270)\to K\bar K$ |
| $f_0(1370)$ | $J/\psi\to\omega f_0(1370)$ production LS variables, fixed mass and width | Full-chain complex totals for $\pi\pi$ and $K\bar K$ decays |
| $f_0(1500)$ | $J/\psi\to\omega f_0(1500)$ production LS variables, fixed mass and width | Full-chain complex totals for $\pi\pi$ and $K\bar K$ decays |

Parameter accounting from the imported model inventory:

| Scope | Total | Floating | Fixed or derived |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Shared by both channels | 32 | 18 | 14 |
| $\omega\pi^+\pi^-$ only | 124 | 46 | 78 |
| $\omega K^+K^-$ only | 114 | 44 | 70 |
| **Combined** | **270** | **108** | **162** |

The other active families are not shared across final states in this fit:
$f_0(500)$, $f_2(2300)$, the $\omega\pi$ isobars and the $\pi\pi$
nonresonant term appear only in $\omega\pi^+\pi^-$; $f_2(1525)$,
$f_0(1710)$, $f_2(2150)$, the $\omega K$ isobars and the $K\bar K$
nonresonant term appear only in $\omega K^+K^-$. Charge-conjugate
$\omega\pi$ and $\omega K$ partners are tied through `coef_head` and
explicit `var_equal` relations.

## Optimizer and resource diagnostics

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-6}
![](assets/generated/optimizer_nll.png){fig-alt="NLL values from complete optimizer outcomes"}

Green points denote converged optimizer records; red points denote records that
did not report convergence.
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-6}
![](assets/generated/memory_growth.png){fig-alt="Resident memory growth during the serial fit"}

Resident memory grew throughout the serial process and reached approximately
18.2 GiB. Future batches should split starts into independent jobs or explicitly
release repeated fit state.
:::
:::

## Fit outcome

Diagonal fit fractions include interference and need not sum to one. Their
sums are 1.586 for $\omega\pi^+\pi^-$ and 1.097 for $\omega K^+K^-$. The
large $\omega\pi\pi$ sum indicates substantial destructive interference.

| Channel | Leading diagonal fractions |
|---|---|
| $\omega\pi^+\pi^-$ | $f_2(1270)$ 34.5%, $f_2(2300)$ 24.5%, NR $2^+$ 20.0%, $f_0(500)$ 16.1%, about 14.4% for each $b_1(1235)^\pm$ chain |
| $\omega K^+K^-$ | $f_0(980)$ 35.1%, $f_0(1710)$ 15.7%, $f_2(2150)$ 13.2%, NR $0^+$ 11.1%, $f_2(1270)$ 8.1% |

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-6}
![](assets/generated/fit_fractions_channel0.png){fig-alt="Largest omega pi pi diagonal fit fractions"}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-6}
![](assets/generated/fit_fractions_channel1.png){fig-alt="Largest omega K K diagonal fit fractions"}
:::
:::

## Main projections

::: {.panel-tabset}
### $\omega\pi^+\pi^-$

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-4}
![](assets/figures/s0_m_pipi.png){fig-alt="pi pi invariant-mass projection"}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-4}
![](assets/figures/s0_m_bp.png){fig-alt="omega pi plus invariant-mass projection"}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-4}
![](assets/figures/s0_m_bm.png){fig-alt="omega pi minus invariant-mass projection"}
:::
:::

### $\omega K^+K^-$

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-4}
![](assets/figures/s1_m_kk.png){fig-alt="K K invariant-mass projection"}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-4}
![](assets/figures/s1_m_kstp.png){fig-alt="omega K plus invariant-mass projection"}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-4}
![](assets/figures/s1_m_kstm.png){fig-alt="omega K minus invariant-mass projection"}
:::
:::
:::

## Dalitz diagnostics

### $\omega\pi^+\pi^-$

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-4}
![](assets/figures/s0_dalitz_omegaPip_PipPim_data_hist.png){fig-alt="omega pi pi Dalitz data"}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-4}
![](assets/figures/s0_dalitz_omegaPip_PipPim_fitted.png){fig-alt="omega pi pi fitted Dalitz distribution"}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-4}
![](assets/figures/s0_dalitz_omegaPip_PipPim_pull.png){fig-alt="omega pi pi Dalitz pull"}
:::
:::

### $\omega K^+K^-$

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-4}
![](assets/figures/s1_dalitz_omegaKp_KpKm_data_hist.png){fig-alt="omega K K Dalitz data"}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-4}
![](assets/figures/s1_dalitz_omegaKp_KpKm_fitted.png){fig-alt="omega K K fitted Dalitz distribution"}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-4}
![](assets/figures/s1_dalitz_omegaKp_KpKm_pull.png){fig-alt="omega K K Dalitz pull"}
:::
:::

## Fit quality

This snapshot is technically successful: the GPU-fixed job completed normally,
the selected optimizer record converged, the selected NLL is the best recovered
minimum, and the page contains the expected optimizer, memory, fit-fraction,
mass-projection and Dalitz diagnostic figures.

The weak points are still limiting. Only 10 of the 20 requested starts left
complete optimizer summaries, two of those reported precision loss, and the
converged minima remain separated by tens to hundreds of NLL units. The large
$\omega\pi\pi$ diagonal-fraction sum means the interpretation is strongly
interference dominated. No binned $\chi^2$, full covariance matrix, closure
result or systematic variation is available here, so this should remain a
working reference rather than a quoted physics result.

## Full figure gallery

This is the complete imported selected-fit gallery: mass and angular
projections, Dalitz data/fitted/pull triplets, and component legends for both
channels.

### $\omega\pi^+\pi^-$ channel
::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s0_Jpsi_bp_pip_alpha.png>){fig-alt="omega pi pi Jpsi bp pip alpha"}

`s0_Jpsi_bp_pip_alpha.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s0_Jpsi_bp_pip_cos(beta).png>){fig-alt="omega pi pi Jpsi bp pip cos(beta)"}

`s0_Jpsi_bp_pip_cos(beta).png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s0_Jpsi_pipi_pip_cos(beta).png>){fig-alt="omega pi pi Jpsi pipi pip cos(beta)"}

`s0_Jpsi_pipi_pip_cos(beta).png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s0_dalitz_omegaPim_PipPim_data_hist.png>){fig-alt="omega pi pi dalitz omegaPim PipPim data hist"}

`s0_dalitz_omegaPim_PipPim_data_hist.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s0_dalitz_omegaPim_PipPim_fitted.png>){fig-alt="omega pi pi dalitz omegaPim PipPim fitted"}

`s0_dalitz_omegaPim_PipPim_fitted.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s0_dalitz_omegaPim_PipPim_pull.png>){fig-alt="omega pi pi dalitz omegaPim PipPim pull"}

`s0_dalitz_omegaPim_PipPim_pull.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s0_dalitz_omegaPip_PipPim_data_hist.png>){fig-alt="omega pi pi dalitz omegaPip PipPim data hist"}

`s0_dalitz_omegaPip_PipPim_data_hist.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s0_dalitz_omegaPip_PipPim_fitted.png>){fig-alt="omega pi pi dalitz omegaPip PipPim fitted"}

`s0_dalitz_omegaPip_PipPim_fitted.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s0_dalitz_omegaPip_PipPim_pull.png>){fig-alt="omega pi pi dalitz omegaPip PipPim pull"}

`s0_dalitz_omegaPip_PipPim_pull.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s0_dalitz_omegaPip_omegaPim_data_hist.png>){fig-alt="omega pi pi dalitz omegaPip omegaPim data hist"}

`s0_dalitz_omegaPip_omegaPim_data_hist.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s0_dalitz_omegaPip_omegaPim_fitted.png>){fig-alt="omega pi pi dalitz omegaPip omegaPim fitted"}

`s0_dalitz_omegaPip_omegaPim_fitted.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s0_dalitz_omegaPip_omegaPim_pull.png>){fig-alt="omega pi pi dalitz omegaPip omegaPim pull"}

`s0_dalitz_omegaPip_omegaPim_pull.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s0_legend.png>){fig-alt="omega pi pi legend"}

`s0_legend.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s0_m_bm.png>){fig-alt="omega pi pi m bm"}

`s0_m_bm.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s0_m_bp.png>){fig-alt="omega pi pi m bp"}

`s0_m_bp.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s0_m_pipi.png>){fig-alt="omega pi pi m pipi"}

`s0_m_pipi.png`
:::
:::


### $\omega K^+K^-$ channel
::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s1_Jpsi_kk_kp_cos(beta).png>){fig-alt="omega K K Jpsi kk kp cos(beta)"}

`s1_Jpsi_kk_kp_cos(beta).png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s1_Jpsi_kstp_kp_alpha.png>){fig-alt="omega K K Jpsi kstp kp alpha"}

`s1_Jpsi_kstp_kp_alpha.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s1_Jpsi_kstp_kp_cos(beta).png>){fig-alt="omega K K Jpsi kstp kp cos(beta)"}

`s1_Jpsi_kstp_kp_cos(beta).png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s1_dalitz_omegaKm_KpKm_data_hist.png>){fig-alt="omega K K dalitz omegaKm KpKm data hist"}

`s1_dalitz_omegaKm_KpKm_data_hist.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s1_dalitz_omegaKm_KpKm_fitted.png>){fig-alt="omega K K dalitz omegaKm KpKm fitted"}

`s1_dalitz_omegaKm_KpKm_fitted.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s1_dalitz_omegaKm_KpKm_pull.png>){fig-alt="omega K K dalitz omegaKm KpKm pull"}

`s1_dalitz_omegaKm_KpKm_pull.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s1_dalitz_omegaKp_KpKm_data_hist.png>){fig-alt="omega K K dalitz omegaKp KpKm data hist"}

`s1_dalitz_omegaKp_KpKm_data_hist.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s1_dalitz_omegaKp_KpKm_fitted.png>){fig-alt="omega K K dalitz omegaKp KpKm fitted"}

`s1_dalitz_omegaKp_KpKm_fitted.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s1_dalitz_omegaKp_KpKm_pull.png>){fig-alt="omega K K dalitz omegaKp KpKm pull"}

`s1_dalitz_omegaKp_KpKm_pull.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s1_dalitz_omegaKp_omegaKm_data_hist.png>){fig-alt="omega K K dalitz omegaKp omegaKm data hist"}

`s1_dalitz_omegaKp_omegaKm_data_hist.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s1_dalitz_omegaKp_omegaKm_fitted.png>){fig-alt="omega K K dalitz omegaKp omegaKm fitted"}

`s1_dalitz_omegaKp_omegaKm_fitted.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s1_dalitz_omegaKp_omegaKm_pull.png>){fig-alt="omega K K dalitz omegaKp omegaKm pull"}

`s1_dalitz_omegaKp_omegaKm_pull.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s1_legend.png>){fig-alt="omega K K legend"}

`s1_legend.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s1_m_kk.png>){fig-alt="omega K K m kk"}

`s1_m_kk.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s1_m_kstm.png>){fig-alt="omega K K m kstm"}

`s1_m_kstm.png`
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-xl-4}
![](<assets/figures/s1_m_kstp.png>){fig-alt="omega K K m kstp"}

`s1_m_kstp.png`
:::
:::


## Next adjustments

1. Import and inspect the reconstructed-PHSP-matched closure cluster `127055`
   outputs. It was submitted on 2026-06-22; `condor_q 127055` showed no active
   jobs on 2026-06-23, so the next action is to check the logs and result
   files rather than assume it is still queued.
2. Require the truth-start closure fit to recover generated projections and
   identifiable parameters, then run independent toy seeds for pull coverage.
3. Compute a full numerical Hessian/covariance and inspect correlations,
   eigenvalues and boundary solutions. The current fraction errors are not a
   final statistical uncertainty statement.
4. Supply genuine no-efficiency PHSP for both channels before quoting
   physics-level fit fractions; the current normalization folds detector
   acceptance into the reported fractions.
5. Run resonance-removal and alternative-line-shape fits. Report
   $\Delta\mathrm{NLL}$, the change in degrees of freedom and whether the
   result is stable under repeated multi-start scans.
6. Split future starts into independent jobs to improve throughput and memory
   isolation, then promote only a closure-checked and systematic-checked result
   to a new dated page.
